div.p: Scalar Autograd Engine Core Data Structure

ul.li: out: reference to output Value For Addition (+): python def _backward(): self.grad += out.grad # ∂(a+b)/∂a = 1 other.grad += out.grad # ∂(a+b)/∂b = 1

div.p: For Multiplication (*): python def _backward(): self.grad += other.data * out.grad other.grad += self.data * out.grad
Key Invariant: Gradients accumulate with += When a Value is used multiple times: python a = Value(3) b = a + a # Both operands reference same object b._backward() # a.grad += 1 twice → a.grad = 2
This is correct: d(2a)/da = 2 WRONG: Using = instead of += would overwrite!

Value object (~56 bytes base + fields) ├── PyObject_HEAD (16 bytes) ├── dict pointer (8 bytes) └── Instance dict entries: ├── 'data': PyFloatObject* (24 bytes) ├── 'grad': PyFloatObject* (24 bytes) ├── '_prev': PySetObject* (~72 bytes + 8/element) ├── '_op': PyUnicodeObject* (~50 bytes) └── '_backward': PyFunctionObject* (~140 bytes) Total: ~350-400 bytes per Value
Computational Graph Edge (via _prev): For c = a + b:
Backward traversal follows _prev edges in reverse topological order.

g.title: The actual scalar value being computed
Set of parent Value objects that produced this
Power: out = self ** n (n must be int/float)
